theory.canonical_statement: onfirm that timedependent modulations follow the predicted rotation frequency. 2.0.155 ID 304: Helical Transfer Function with Berry and Sagnac Phases Type: Operator Formal Statement: For orbital angular momentum (OAM) mode mmm at rotation frequency Ω\OmegaΩ in a helical waveguide, the transfer function is $Hm(Ω,t)=exp[i(ωmt+ΦmBerry+ΦmSagnac(Ω))]H_m(\Omega,t)=\exp\bigl[i(\omega_m$ t + \Phiˆ{\text{Berry}} m + $\Phiˆ{\text{Sagnac}} m(\Omega))\bigr]Hm(Ω,t)=exp[i(ωmt+ΦmBerry+ΦmSagnac(Ω))],$ where $ωm=ckz2+(m/R)2+2mΩ/n\omega_m$ = c\sqrt{k_zˆ2 + (m/R)ˆ2 + $2m\Omega/n}ωm$ = $ckz2$ + $(m/R)2$ + $2mΩ/n,ΦmBerry$ = $(2lnφ/π)mt\PhiΘ{\text{Berry}} m$ = $(2\ln\varphi/\pi)mtΦmBerry$ = $(2lnφ/π)mt, andΦmSagnac(Ω)$ = $(4πΩR2/λ0c)m\Phiˆ{\text{Sagnac}} m(\Omega)$ = (4\pi\Omega Rˆ2/\lambda_0 c) $mΦmSagnac(Ω)=(4πΩR2/λ0c)m.$ Interpretive Meaning: Light propagating through a rotating helical medium accumulates both a geometrical

It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from c, k, z, m, R, n, defining ωm.
